title: Launch jurisdiction-scope decision (neutral core confirmed; overlay criteria + deadline)
context: One of the most common failure modes of financial-literacy content is presenting one country's money system as universal (e.g., US credit scores, 401(k)s, UK ISAs) without labeling. The project must decide its launch jurisdiction scope in M0 — before any content is authored — to prevent this. The default is a jurisdiction-neutral core. The question is whether and which single jurisdiction overlay to add at launch, and this decision must be grounded in open-source availability, credentialed local reviewer availability, audience size, and tractable complexity.
objective: Research and record the M0 jurisdiction-scope decision: confirm jurisdiction-neutral core as the default launch scope, define the criteria and M0 deadline for selecting a first overlay jurisdiction, and document the decision rationale in governance/.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirms jurisdiction-neutral core as the launch scope with a clear rationale recorded

Defines the four scoring criteria for choosing a first overlay jurisdiction: (1) availability of public-domain/openly-licensed authoritative sources, (2) availability of a local credentialed reviewer, (3) size of an underserved, low-literacy audience reachable via a partner, (4) tractable consumer-finance complexity
Records that the specific overlay jurisdiction remains 'TO BE SECURED' pending a local reviewer, and sets the M0 decision deadline (2026-08-31 per PLAN.md)
Identifies candidate overlay jurisdictions ranked by the four criteria, with notes on open-source availability and reviewer availability for each candidate
Document is reviewed and approved by the maintainer (and the credentialed financial educator when engaged)
output: A governance document (governance/jurisdiction-scope-decision.md) recording the jurisdiction-neutral core decision, overlay scoring criteria, candidate jurisdictions, and M0 deadline — the rule and deadline are fixed; the specific overlay remains TO BE SECURED.
